name: Thingyan (Water Festival)
traditions: Water dousing and water fights; mandats (water-throwing stages); religious observances; washing Buddha images; hair washing of elders.
historicalBackground: Thingyan marks the Myanmar New Year and has Buddhist origins. Water symbolizes the cleansing of sins from the old year.
description: Myanmar's exuberant water festival celebrating the Buddhist New Year, where cities transform into massive water-soaked celebrations.
